records.ety: [F. disposition, dispositio, fr. disponere to dispose; dis- + ponere to place. See Position, and cf. Dispone.]

defs: The act of disposing, arranging, ordering, regulating, or transferring; application; disposal; as, the disposition of a man's property by will.
quotes: Who have received the law by the disposition of angels.
The disposition of the work, to put all things in a beautiful order and harmony, that the whole may be of a piece.

defs: The state or the manner of being disposed or arranged; distribution; arrangement; order; as, the disposition of the trees in an orchard; the disposition of the several parts of an edifice.
Tendency to any action or state resulting from natural constitution; nature; quality; as, a disposition in plants to grow in a direction upward; a disposition in bodies to putrefaction.

quotes: How stands your disposition to be married?

defs: Natural or prevailing spirit, or temperament of mind, especially as shown in intercourse with one's fellow-men; temper of mind.
quotes: His disposition led him to do things agreeable to his quality and condition wherein God had placed him.
As I perchance hereafter shall think meet To put an antic disposition on.
synonyms: Temper. Disposition is the natural humor of a person
the predominating quality of his character
the constitutional habit of his mind. Character is this disposition influenced by motive
and will. Temper is a quality of the fiber of character
and is displayed chiefly when the emotions
